component.name: StatBlock
component.description: Chromeless stat layout primitive. Composes label, value, icon, description, secondary text, and trend indicator with no container chrome. Designed to be placed inside any surface — Card, MetricPanel, MetricStripItem, or custom containers.

useCases: Any scenario that displays a labeled metric — wrap StatBlock in the appropriate container for the context
Inside a Card for an elevated, standalone KPI with optional footer for a short note or action
Inside MetricPanel tab triggers for expandable metric rows
Inside MetricStripItem for richer content than the default value/label layout
Inside custom surfaces like sidebars, popovers, or inline sections where card chrome is unwanted

commonPatterns.name: in-card-simple
commonPatterns.description: StatBlock inside a Card — the standard elevated KPI pattern. Card owns the chrome, StatBlock owns the text layout.
commonPatterns.composition: <Card level={1}><CardContent><StatBlock label="Revenue" value="$12,400" trend={{ value: "+12.5%", direction: "up" }} /></CardContent></Card>

commonPatterns.name: in-card-with-footer-note
commonPatterns.description: Card with a footer containing a short contextual note. Footer text should be a very short sentence using p-sm muted text.
commonPatterns.composition: vs last period

commonPatterns.name: in-card-with-footer-action
commonPatterns.description: Card with a footer action. Use a ghost xs Button for the action.
commonPatterns.composition: <Card level={1}><CardContent><StatBlock label="Open Issues" value="24" /></CardContent><CardFooter data-divider className="bg-secondary"><Button variant="ghost" size="xs">View all</Button></CardFooter></Card>

commonPatterns.name: in-card-with-footer-note-and-action
commonPatterns.description: Card with both a short note and an action in the footer.
commonPatterns.composition: vs last period

commonPatterns.name: in-card-large
commonPatterns.description: Spacious layout with size lg for hero metrics. Good for prominent dashboard KPIs.
commonPatterns.composition: <Card level={1}><CardContent><StatBlock size="lg" valueSize="lg" icon={<DollarSign className="size-5" />} label="Total Revenue" value="$482,320" description="Year to date" secondary="Includes all approved transactions" /></CardContent></Card>

commonPatterns.name: auto-fill-grid
commonPatterns.description: Responsive grid of stat Cards using CSS auto-fill. Cards flow naturally based on container width — no fixed column count.

commonPatterns.name: chromeless
commonPatterns.description: StatBlock without any container — for inline use in sidebars, popovers, or custom layouts.

commonPatterns.name: in-metric-strip-item
commonPatterns.description: StatBlock inside a MetricStripItem for richer content than simple value/label.
commonPatterns.composition: <MetricStripItem><StatBlock label="Revenue" value="$12,400" valueSize="sm" trend={{ value: "+6.2%", direction: "up" }} /></MetricStripItem>

antiPatterns.scenario: Adding border, background, or shadow directly to StatBlock
antiPatterns.reason: StatBlock is intentionally chromeless. Container chrome belongs on the wrapping surface (Card, MetricStrip, etc.), not on StatBlock itself.
antiPatterns.alternative: Wrap StatBlock in a Card or another container component.

antiPatterns.scenario: Reimplementing the label/value/trend layout in a custom div
antiPatterns.reason: StatBlock already standardizes this composition. Reimplementing it leads to inconsistent typography, spacing, and trend treatment.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use StatBlock and control the container separately.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using footer for long text or multiple paragraphs
antiPatterns.reason: The CardFooter on a stat card is for a very short note (one short sentence, p-sm muted) or a single action (ghost xs Button). Long content belongs in the card body or a separate section.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use description or secondary props for explanatory text within the StatBlock layout.

semanticMeaning.default: Compact layout with gap-1 between label and value rows. Use for most stat displays.
semanticMeaning.lg: Spacious layout with gap-6 between label and value rows. Use for hero metrics or prominent dashboard KPIs.

slots.icon: Optional ReactNode rendered top-right, aligned with the label. Use muted-foreground icons at size-5.
slots.label: Primary metric label. Uses label-sm by default, label-md at size lg.
slots.description: Muted helper text under the label (p-sm text-muted-foreground).
slots.value: The primary metric string. Sized via valueSize: data-sm, data-md (default), or data-lg.
slots.secondary: ReactNode below the value. Used for explainer text or trend copy (p text-muted-foreground).
slots.trend: Object with value string, direction ('up' | 'down'), and optional tone. Renders a ChevronUp/Down icon + text. Tone defaults to direction-inferred (up→success, down→destructive).
slots.trendPosition: 'inline' (default) — side by side with value; 'between' — value left, trend right, full width; 'below' — trend on its own line.

parentConstraints: StatBlock does not provide its own padding — the parent container handles padding
When inside a Card, use CardContent for padding and optionally CardFooter for notes/actions

responsive.layout: StatBlock does not own grid layout. For grids of stat Cards, use CSS auto-fill on the parent: grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(14rem,1fr))]. Cards adapt to container width without breakpoint cliffs.

accessibility.role: Presentational container; trend tone conveyed via text color
accessibility.screenReader: Label, value, trend value, description, and secondary text are all readable in DOM order; icons should carry aria-label if meaningful

rules: StatBlock is chromeless — never add border, background, or shadow to it directly; wrap it in the appropriate container
The parent container owns padding; StatBlock has none
For elevated standalone KPIs, wrap in Card with CardContent
CardFooter on a stat uses bg-secondary and data-divider — only for a very short note (p-sm muted, one short sentence) or a single action (ghost xs Button), not for long content
Use size='lg' for hero/prominent metrics, size='default' for everything else
valueSize controls the metric text scale independently from the layout density (size)
For grids, use grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(14rem,1fr))] on the parent div — the page owns the grid, StatBlock owns the text layout
alternatives: MetricStrip for related metrics that should be visually de-emphasized in a single surface
MetricPanel for stat rows that expand to show detailed content
LabelValue for simple label-value pairs without trend or icon support

aiHints.context: StatBlock is the chromeless primitive for displaying a labeled metric with optional icon, description, secondary text, and trend indicator. It never renders its own container chrome — wrap it in Card (for elevated KPIs), MetricStripItem (for compact related metrics), or any custom surface. For grids of stat cards, use a plain div with grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(14rem,1fr))]. Card owns the surface; StatBlock owns the text layout.
